verdict_reasoning: KLK2 (hK2) is a secreted trypsin-like serine protease of the kallikrein family, produced by prostatic epithelium and released into seminal fluid and serum where it activates pro-PSA. It has a signal peptide but no TM domain, GPI anchor, or outer-leaflet lipidation. Checking contextual buckets: no documented cancer/cell-death/activation ecto-pool of the protein body (prostate-tumor relevance is via secreted enzymatic activity and PSMA-axis biology, not surface display); no narrow germline/developmental surface display with its own anchor; not a lysosomal TM protein; no documented stable PM minority pool or trafficking cycling with anchor; no wash-resistant covalent/non-covalent tethering to a TM partner. Clinical interest (e.g., radioligand programs) targets the secreted enzyme, not a surface pool.
